findings.title: Started critical sections still inherit later submitter cancellation
findings.detail: Submit documents that once a critical section has begun it runs to completion regardless of later ctx cancellation, but execute passes the original submitter context directly into critical. If the per-run context is cancelled after the pre-execution gate, any git/ref/working-tree command inside the critical section can observe cancellation and abort mid-phase, which defeats the merge queue's job of protecting local ref and worktree mutation windows.
findings.suggested_fix: After the pre-execution cancellation gate, invoke critical with a cancellation-detached context, for example context.WithoutCancel(j.ctx), or with an explicit merge-domain context whose lifetime is independent of the per-run context. Add a test where critical blocks until after Submit's ctx is cancelled and then verifies the context passed to critical is still usable.

findings.title: Queue shutdown can still admit one more critical section
findings.detail: The owner loop selects between ctx.Done and q.submit. Once the Start context is cancelled, both cases can be ready; Go select may choose the submit receive, so a submitter blocked during shutdown can enter and run critical instead of getting ErrQueueStopped. That contradicts Submit's stopped-queue contract and can run merge-domain work after the daemon has begun shutting the queue down.
findings.suggested_fix: After receiving a job, check the owner context before executing; if it is cancelled, send ErrQueueStopped to that job and return. Alternatively structure shutdown so cancellation closes admission before the owner can receive more work.

findings.title: Strict FIFO depends on Go runtime blocked-sender ordering
findings.detail: RSM-015 requires FIFO ordering among concurrent submissions, but the implementation relies on the unbuffered channel's blocked-sender queue being FIFO. The Go language contract does not define fairness or sender wake order for multiple concurrent senders, so the merge ordering guarantee rests on runtime behavior rather than package logic.
findings.suggested_fix: Assign an explicit monotonically increasing sequence number under a mutex at Submit entry, enqueue jobs into a heap/list owned by the queue, and have the owner execute by sequence. Then test truly concurrent submissions against the explicit sequence, not sleep-shaped sender blocking.

findings.title: Invariant harness blesses git push inside the merge exclusion domain
findings.detail: RSM-017 says the conformance test must assert no git push runs inside the merge queue exclusive section, but commitAllowlist includes "git push" and the fixture records it inside Submit. This test would pass if the production critical section regressed to holding the queue across the network push.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove "git push" from commitAllowlist, make it explicitly forbidden inside the domain, and update the fixture so push is recorded outside Queue.Submit.
